description_long: This is an ornate, circular cup with a delicate, openwork design featuring intricate gold scrollwork patterns around its rim. The cup appears to be made primarily of a thin metallic band, likely brass or gold-plated, forming both the rim and the decorative motifs. The body of the cup is largely open, with no solid walls, giving it a lightweight and airy appearance. The scrollwork is symmetrical and evenly spaced, contributing to an elegant and decorative aesthetic. The base of the cup is not visible, suggesting it may be minimal or absent, further emphasizing the ornamental nature. This cup would likely serve a decorative or ceremonial function rather than practical everyday use.
description: An ornate, gold-toned cup with intricate openwork scroll patterns and a delicate, airy structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical gold scrollwork and the open, circular rim.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the continuity of the scroll pattern and the thinness of the rim.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the mirrored scroll motifs and the open, unobstructed body.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the three-dimensionality and the delicate, filigree design.
